items.headline: City launches amnesty program for unlicensed businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program with outreach to shopping centers, likely allowing businesses operating without proper licensing to come into compliance without penalties. This could affect how the city enforces business regulations going forward.

items.headline: New incentives coming for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city council discussed economic development incentives specifically targeting retail shops, small businesses, and startups. Details on what these incentives are (tax breaks, grants, expedited permits) would indicate whether this meaningfully affects the local business climate.

items.headline: City announces CDBG funding applications being considered
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is reviewing Community Development Block Grant applications on March 25. These federal funds typically support affordable housing, social services, and community development projects that directly affect local residents.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading, meaning it's now moving forward. This project will add housing to Mountain View, addressing the area's ongoing housing shortage.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides state funding to cities that remove barriers to housing development. This could bring state money to help accelerate housing projects in Mountain View.

items.headline: City seeking public input on how to spend HUD housing funds
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on its draft plan for federal housing grant money for fiscal year 2026/27. This is an opportunity for residents to influence priorities for affordable housing and community development projects in the city.
